target_files.legacy-to-mcp3/input-documents/petclinic: Quarkus REST client interface (MicroProfile REST Client) that mirrors GET /api/pets and GET /api/pets/{petId} from the PetClinic OpenAPI spec; annotated with @RegisterRestClient and @Path("/api")
POJO / record representing the Pet schema from the OpenAPI spec (id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, visits)
POJO / record representing the PetType sub-schema (id, name)

Quarkus MCP server bean (@ApplicationScoped) exposing two @Tool methods: listPets() and getPetById(@ToolArg petId); both return ToolResponse wrapping TextContent (JSON serialization of Pet payloads); imports from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server only
Quarkus application configuration: quarkus.http.port=8888, quarkus.http.cors=true, REST client base URL pointing at the PetClinic service (http://spring-petclinic-rest:9966)
Kubernetes manifest updated to add the petclinic-mcp-bob container as a sidecar in the existing spring-petclinic-rest Deployment; sidecar exposes containerPort 8888

modernization_steps.task: Verify pom.xml dependencies: quarkus-mcp-server-http and quarkus-rest-client-jackson are already present — do NOT modify pom.xml

Create Pet.java POJO/record with fields: int id, String name, LocalDate birthDate, PetType type, int ownerId, List<Visit> visits (visits list can be omitted or typed as List<Object> if Visit schema is out of scope)
Create PetType.java POJO/record with fields: int id, String name

Create PetRestClient.java: interface annotated with @Path("/api") and @RegisterRestClient(configKey="petclinic"); define @GET @Path("/pets") List<Pet> listPets() and @GET @Path("/pets/{petId}") Pet getPetById(@PathParam("petId") int petId)

Create PetMcpServer.java: @ApplicationScoped CDI bean; inject PetRestClient; implement @Tool(description="List all pets") ToolResponse listPets() and @Tool(description="Get a pet by ID") ToolResponse getPetById(@ToolArg(name="petId", description="The ID of the pet") int petId); serialize results via Jackson ObjectMapper to JSON string; wrap in new ToolResponse(List.of(new TextContent(json))); all imports from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server.*

Update petclinic-kubernetes.yaml: add sidecar container named petclinic-mcp in the spring-petclinic-rest Deployment spec; use image placeholder (e.g. org.acme/petclinic-mcp-bob:latest); add containerPort 8888; expose sidecar via the existing Service or a new Service on port 8888

constraints: Do NOT modify legacy application source code
Only expose GET operations: GET /api/pets and GET /api/pets/{petId}
MCP server must run on port 8888 (quarkus.http.port=8888)
Use @Tool and @ToolArg annotations from io.quarkiverse.mcp.server only — NOT LangChain4j
Tool methods must return ToolResponse containing TextContent
Do NOT modify pom.xml — quarkus-mcp-server-http and quarkus-rest-client-jackson are already present
All new Java source files go under legacy-to-mcp3/input-documents/petclinic-mcp-bob/src/main/java/org/acme/
Legacy PetClinic REST service runs on port 9966 (from Kubernetes manifest)
Quarkus platform version is 3.32.1 with Java 25 compiler target
evaluation_criteria: PetRestClient interface compiles and correctly maps GET /api/pets and GET /api/pets/{petId} from the OpenAPI spec
Pet and PetType POJOs/records match the OpenAPI schema fields (id, name, birthDate, type, ownerId, visits)
PetMcpServer exposes exactly two @Tool methods — listPets and getPetById — with no LangChain4j imports
ToolResponse wraps TextContent containing valid JSON representation of Pet payloads
REST client base URL in application.properties resolves to the Kubernetes service spring-petclinic-rest on port 9966
petclinic-kubernetes.yaml Deployment contains sidecar container exposing port 8888
mvn quarkus:dev starts the MCP server on port 8888 without errors
MCP tool calls to listPets and getPetById return correctly structured JSON responses
pom.xml is unmodified from its original state
